title: Arundinella hordeiformis
taxonomy: Arundinella hordeiformis is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Arundinella, which consists of several species of grasses. Arundinella hordeiformis is native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, and is often found in open grasslands and savannas. The plant is an annual grass that grows up to 1-2 meters in height, and is characterized by its slender stems and long, narrow leaves. Arundinella hordeiformis is often used as a forage crop in some parts of the world due to its high nutritional value.

It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
